title: SAF Repels Major RSF Multi-Front Assault on Dalang, South Kordofan
detail: RSF forces launched a large-scale multi-front assault on Dalang in the Nuba Mountains of South Kordofan on April 2, mobilizing fighters alongside SPLM-N allied elements and South Sudanese mercenaries in an attempt to open a new front in the contested state. The RSF briefly penetrated Dalang's outer neighborhoods before SAF forces, supported by local allied militias, successfully repelled the attack and pushed the RSF back to surrounding positions. SAF destroyed several RSF fighting vehicles during the engagement. Analysts noted that tribal tensions within RSF ranks complicated the assault: RSF units recruited from Arab tribes with historical rivalries against the Nuba (who form the backbone of SPLM-N forces) reportedly refused to participate in close ground operations against Nuba fighters, weakening RSF assault cohesion. The attack on Dalang opened simultaneously with continued pressure on Dilling and Blue Nile State, indicating a coordinated RSF-SPLM-N strategy to stretch SAF defenses across multiple South Kordofan fronts.

title: SAF General Burhan Reshuffles Top Military Command; Gen. Atta Appointed Chief of Staff
detail: SAF Commander-in-Chief Gen. Abdel Fattah al-Burhan carried out a significant reshuffle of Sudan's top military leadership on April 2, appointing a new army chief of staff. The appointment of Gen. Atta to the chief of staff position came amid intense multi-front pressure on SAF positions across South Kordofan and Blue Nile State. Command reshuffles in Sudanese military history have often signaled either preparations for major offensives or responses to battlefield setbacks. The reshuffle followed a week of intense RSF-SPLM-N pressure on Dilling, failed peace talks in March, and growing questions about SAF's ability to hold multiple contested fronts simultaneously. No further details on additional command changes were immediately available.
